file_insights.changes: Added Session 3 completion record documenting zsh completions verification
Updated subtask-1-3 status from pending to completed, added timestamp, updated subtask-1-2 notes with detailed verification info

patterns_discovered.name: verification_only_tasks
patterns_discovered.description: Both subtask-1-2 and subtask-1-3 are smoke test verification tasks requiring no code changes

patterns_discovered.name: output_format_validation
patterns_discovered.description: Verification pattern: run CLI command and validate output starts with expected header (#compdef bop for zsh, _bop() for bash)

patterns_discovered.name: json_array_formatting
patterns_discovered.description: Implementation plan JSON shows conversion of single-line arrays to multi-line with proper indentation

patterns_discovered.name: timestamp_tracking
patterns_discovered.description: Updated_at fields added with ISO 8601 timestamps to track completion time

gotchas_discovered.description: Verification only checks if output starts with expected pattern (e.g., '#compdef bop'), not full script validity or functionality
gotchas_discovered.impact: May miss malformed completion scripts that have correct header

gotchas_discovered.description: Verification tasks marked complete without any code changes, reducing signal for actual implementation correctness
gotchas_discovered.impact: Subtasks 1-2 and 1-3 don't validate implementation details, only output format

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple unrelated JSON formatting changes (array indentation, final newline) mixed with functional updates
gotchas_discovered.impact: Makes diff harder to read, potential for accidental formatting changes during updates

approach_outcome.validation_result: zsh completion script verified - output starts with '#compdef bop' as expected

recommendations.suggestion: Extend verification beyond header format check to validate complete script syntax (e.g., zsh -n for syntax check)
Add integration tests that actually load completion scripts in shells to verify functionality, not just format
Normalize JSON formatting as separate concern from functional updates; consider using formatter tool (prettier/jq) before committing
Use consistent timestamp format and precision across all metadata updates; current mix of microseconds and zeros may indicate manual edits
For shell completion features, add actual shell integration tests rather than just script output validation
